task_completion.description: The original 28 cm body tube is replaced by two separate body tubes ('Fore' and 'Aft'), each approximately 14 cm in length.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the OpenRocket component tree or design view, there should be two distinct body tubes visible instead of one. They should ideally be named 'Fore' and 'Aft' (or similar), and their lengths should be approximately 14 cm each, visible in the configuration dialog or visually proportional in the side view.

task_completion.description: A 'Tube Coupler' is present, joining the Fore and Aft body tubes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Tube Coupler' component is visible in the OpenRocket component tree, nested under one of the body tubes, and visually positioned between the two body tubes in the rocket design view.

task_completion.description: The Parachute, Shock Cord, and Nose Cone are housed in the Fore section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the component tree, the Nose Cone is at the top, and the Parachute and Shock Cord are nested underneath the 'Fore' body tube.

task_completion.description: The Fins, Motor Mount (Inner Tube), and Launch Lug are housed in the Aft section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the component tree, the Fins, Inner Tube (representing the Motor Mount), and Launch Lug are nested underneath the 'Aft' body tube.

task_completion.description: The repaired rocket is stable and a simulation shows an apogee exceeding 50m.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The top right corner of the OpenRocket window shows a positive stability margin (e.g., > 1.0 cal). The 'Flight Simulations' tab shows a completed simulation run with an apogee value greater than 50m.

task_completion.description: The modified design is saved to the correct path and a repair report is written documenting mass, stability, and apogee.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenRocket window title or a save dialog shows the file saved as 'spliced_airframe.ork' in '~/Documents/rockets/'. A text editor or terminal shows the creation of 'splice_repair_report.txt' in '~/Documents/exports/' containing the final mass, stability margin, and apogee values.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket design.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenRocket application open with the component tree being actively modified, rather than the agent just editing the .ork XML file directly in a text editor.

integrity.description: The simulation results in the report match the actual simulation run in OpenRocket.
integrity.visual_evidence: The mass, stability, and apogee values written in the text report correspond to the actual values visible in the OpenRocket interface.

integrity.description: The agent actively created and wrote the report file rather than echoing a pre-written string.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano) or a terminal command is visible creating the report file with specific values extracted from the current OpenRocket session.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The modified OpenRocket design must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/spliced_airframe.ork'. The repair report must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/exports/splice_repair_report.txt'. The original file is 'simple_model_rocket.ork'. The passing threshold is 70/100.
